description: Validate AEON clean install, fix test failures, harden install script, add missing .env and LICENSE

userStories.title: Add .env to .gitignore
userStories.description: As a developer, I need .env excluded from version control to prevent leaking secrets.
acceptanceCriteria: Add '.env' and '.env.*' lines to .gitignore
Existing .gitignore patterns are preserved
Typecheck passes (python -c 'import aeon' succeeds)

userStories.title: Add MIT LICENSE file
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a LICENSE file so the project has clear open-source licensing matching the MIT declared in pyproject.toml.
acceptanceCriteria: LICENSE file exists in project root with MIT license text
Copyright holder is set to 'James Walton' with year 2026
License text matches standard MIT template
Typecheck passes (python -c 'import aeon' succeeds)

userStories.title: Fix setup.py dependency list to match actual imports
userStories.description: As a developer, I want pip install -e . to succeed in a fresh venv without missing dependency errors.
acceptanceCriteria: Run pip install -e . in a fresh venv and capture any ImportError or ModuleNotFoundError
All required runtime dependencies are listed in setup.py install_requires
Optional heavy dependencies (llvmlite) are moved to extras_require if they fail to install on some platforms
pip install -e . completes without error
python -c 'from aeon.cli import main' succeeds after install
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Scanned all imports in aeon/. Core CLI uses only stdlib. Moved z3-solver, llvmlite, javalang, flask, flask-limiter, plotly, PyJWT to extras_require. All are behind try/except guards at runtime. pip install -e . now succeeds with zero external deps; pip install -e '.[full]' gets everything.

userStories.title: Fix install.sh broken paths and hardcoded repo URL
userStories.description: As a user, I want bash install.sh to work when run from a local clone, not just from the GitHub URL.
acceptanceCriteria: install.sh detects if it's already inside the AEON repo and skips git clone
If run from inside the repo, uses current directory instead of cloning to ~/.aeon
AEON_REPO URL points to correct GitHub org (Aphrodine-wq, not aeon-lang)
Wrapper script at ~/.local/bin/aeon uses correct paths
install.sh runs without error in a temp directory simulation (dry run check)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fixed AEON_REPO URL to Aphrodine-wq/AEON. Added local repo detection: if setup.py/pyproject.toml + .git exist in cwd, sets AEON_INSTALL_DIR to pwd and skips clone. Wrapper script at ~/.local/bin/aeon correctly references AEON_INSTALL_DIR.

userStories.title: Fix test suite — get all test files passing
userStories.description: As a developer, I want pytest to pass so CI can gate on test results.
acceptanceCriteria: pytest runs from project root without crashing
test_compiler.py passes or has clear skip markers for unimplemented features
test_contracts.py passes or has clear skip markers
test_hoare.py passes or has clear skip markers
test_ai.py passes or has clear skip markers (may need API key skip)
test_perf.py passes or has clear skip markers
test_lattice_laws.py passes or has clear skip markers
No test file produces an unhandled exception
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use pytest.mark.skip or pytest.mark.skipif for tests that require external services (AI APIs) or optional deps (llvmlite). Fix import errors. Fix any broken test assertions.

userStories.title: Document working vs scaffolded language adapters
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a clear record of which language adapters are functional vs stubs.
acceptanceCriteria: Create ADAPTER_STATUS.md in project root
Lists all adapters from aeon/adapters/ directory
Each adapter marked as: Working, Partial, or Scaffolded
Working adapters have been tested with aeon check on a sample file
Includes a test command example for each working adapter
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Read each adapter module in aeon/adapters/. Check if it has real analysis logic or just returns empty results. Test the Python adapter at minimum since that's the primary target.
